frameworks.name: drift-diffusion model
frameworks.one_line: Decomposes choice into evidence accumulation bias vs response caution

frameworks.name: pupil-linked phasic arousal
frameworks.one_line: Pupil dilation indexes transient arousal state modulating decisions

frameworks.name: prior-expectation bias in decision formation
frameworks.one_line: Phasic arousal reduces the impact of prior bias on choices

core_question: Does pupil-linked phasic arousal causally reduce choice bias, or does choosing against bias cause arousal?
key_assumptions: Pupil size reliably indexes phasic arousal
Auditory accessory stimulus elevates phasic arousal independently of the decision
Drift-diffusion decomposition separates accumulation bias from response caution
manipulations: Auditory accessory stimulus vs normal trials
Computational modeling of choice behavior with drift-diffusion
key_findings: Accessory stimulus trials showed elevated pupil-linked phasic arousal
Choice bias was reduced on accessory stimulus trials
Bias reduction was not explained by changes in response caution
Bias reduction reflected a change in evidence-accumulation bias
interpretations: Pupil-linked phasic arousal causally shapes choice behavior by altering accumulation bias
Supports the causal direction from arousal to bias reduction over the reverse account
